refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple requirements or specify implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a real-time communication protocol optimized for low latency and reliability
Design a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow for teleoperation commands and feedback
Use local buffering and predictive control algorithms to compensate for network latency
Segment teleoperation data into critical and non-critical streams to optimize transmission priority
Establish an end-to-end latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanism to maintain latency within limits
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow for teleoperation commands and feedback

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication protocol and control system
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP isolates teleoperation communication as a dedicated channel with prioritization, reducing coupling with other system functions and supporting latency requirements.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Prioritization mechanisms do not starve other critical system communications
Latency monitoring is accurate and responsive enough to trigger adaptations
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency compensation techniques (e.g., predictive control) as a complementary DP to enhance robustness under variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing remote control commands and feedback communication

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional flow of telemetry data including feedback from vehicle to teleoperation system

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional interface for sending teleoperation commands and receiving acknowledgments or status updates

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Underlying network communication channel supporting prioritized data flow for teleoperation

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but with high diffusion and low cohesion due to encompassing many unrelated functions.
Vehicle module is focused on physical vehicle systems; communication channel for teleoperation is only partially contained and would require significant diffusion outside.
Highly cohesive containment of DP; communication channel is core to teleoperation functions with minimal diffusion outside.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; communication channel for teleoperation commands is largely outside its scope.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the dedicated communication channel for prioritized teleoperation commands and feedback, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on underlying Network Link interface may require coordination with broader system modules.
If teleoperation commands require integration with safety alerts, some cross-module communication may be necessary.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a dedicated communication channel with prioritized data flow to meet the latency and safety requirements for teleoperation. This mapping is clear and focused on achieving the end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on underlying Network Link interface may require coordination with broader system modules.
If teleoperation commands require integration with safety alerts, some cross-module communication may be necessary.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module shows high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion of the DP within this IM boundary. This suggests the DP is well contained within the Teleoperation module, minimizing diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by testing the Teleoperation module's communication latency and ensuring it meets the p95 ≤ 150 ms criterion under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Teleoperation IM boundary is clear and specific, with well-defined interfaces supporting prioritized data flow and command/feedback communication.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document coordination mechanisms between the Teleoperation module and the Network Link interface to manage dependencies and ensure latency requirements are met.
Consider defining interface contracts or protocols for any necessary cross-module communication with Safety Services to handle integration of teleoperation commands with safety alerts.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear, focused mappings and strong containment of the DP within the Teleoperation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are established, and diffusion risks are minimal and manageable through documented coordination mechanisms. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition sufficiently addresses the requirements without further sub-FR derivation at this stage.

revise.why: Explicitly document coordination mechanisms between the Teleoperation module and the Network Link interface, and define interface contracts for cross-module communication with Safety Services to manage dependencies and ensure latency and safety requirements.

assumptions: The Teleoperation module can meet the p95 ≤ 150 ms latency under agreed network conditions.
Network Link interface and Safety Services modules are stable and provide required interfaces for coordination.
open_risks: Dependency on Network Link interface may affect latency if not properly coordinated.
Cross-module communication with Safety Services may introduce integration complexity.
evidence_needed: Latency test results of Teleoperation module under realistic network conditions.
Documentation of interface contracts and coordination protocols with Network Link and Safety Services modules.
